2606.29657.title: Safety from Honesty in a Disinterested AI Predictor
2606.29657.paper_summary: As AI systems become more capable, training procedures that optimize for downstream outcomes risk introducing implicit agency: goal-directed behavior that designers never specified. We present a formal safety argument for the Scientist AI (SAI) Predictor, trained to approximate the Bayesian posterior conditioned on a dataset of "epistemically contextualized" natural-language statements. We argue that such a Predictor can honestly predict agents, actions, and their consequences without itself being an agent that selects outputs to achieve goals. This rests on data representation and on the training procedure. Epistemic contextualization of text distinguishes latent factual claims from communication acts, so expressions of goals are treated as evidence to be explained rather than drives the model adopts. With a posterior-seeking training objective, this is intended to drive the Predictor toward calibrated, cautious predictions. Training proceeds so downstream effects of deploying a prediction never serve as a reward signal; any agency the system needs is supplied by explicit scaffolding constrained by guardrails. We prove that, under assumptions on the training dynamics and on the argued sparsity of dangerous Predictors, the probability that training produces a Predictor whose guarded deployment carries residual harm above a specified threshold is small: a dangerous Predictor would have to underestimate harm in a coordinated way across many queries while such coordinated patterns are rare under the initialization distribution and receive no direct training signal. Safety and accuracy are jointly supported in this framework, since the constraints that secure accuracy are the same ones that make coordinated deception costly. These guarantees against misalignment and agency arising from within the Predictor itself do not preclude the use of the Predictor as part of an agentic system.
2606.29657.paper_summary_zh: 隨著 AI 系統變得越來越強大，優化下游結果的訓練程序可能會引入隱含的代理性：設計者從未指定的目標導向行為。我們提出了一個針對科學家 AI (SAI) 預測器的正式安全論證，該預測器的訓練目的是近似於基於“認識論上下文化”的自然語言陳述的貝葉斯後驗。我們主張這樣的預測器可以誠實地預測代理、行動及其後果，而不需要成為一個選擇輸出以達成目標的代理。這依賴於數據表示和訓練程序。文本的認識論上下文化區分潛在的事實主張與溝通行為，因此目標的表達被視為需要解釋的證據，而不是模型所採納的驅動因素。通過尋求後驗的訓練目標，這旨在驅動預測器朝向經過校準的、謹慎的預測。訓練進行時，部署預測的下游效果從不作為獎勵信號；系統所需的任何代理性均由受限於護欄的明確支撐提供。我們證明，在對訓練動力學的假設以及對危險預測器稀疏性的論證下，訓練產生的預測器在受控部署中帶來的殘餘傷害超過指定閾值的概率是小的：一個危險的預測器必須在多個查詢中以協調的方式低估傷害，而這樣的協調模式在初始化分佈下是罕見的，並且沒有直接的訓練信號。在這個框架中，安全性和準確性是共同支持的，因為保障準確性的約束與使協調欺騙代價高昂的約束是相同的。這些針對預測器內部產生的錯位和代理性的保證並不排除將預測器用作代理系統的一部分。
2606.29657.author: Yoshua Bengio et.al.
2606.29657.authors: Yoshua Bengio, Oliver Richardson, Tomáš Gavenčiak, Michael Cohen, Rory Svarc, Damiano Fornasiere, Gael Gendron, David Hyland, Aton Kamanda, Adam Oberman, Francis Rhys Ward, Anna Gavenčiak, Jacob Livingston Slosser, Vincent Mai, Iulian Serban, Joumana Ghosn
